title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: July 02, 2026 - Navigating Jobs Report and Tech Sector Pullback

items.headline: US Equities Show Mixed Performance Amid Weaker Jobs Data and Tech Sell-off
items.summary: US markets are displaying mixed sentiment today, July 2, 2026, as investors digest a weaker-than-expected June jobs report and continued pressure on the technology and semiconductor sectors. The S&P 500 is trading modestly higher, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average also sees gains. However, the Nasdaq Composite is showing a slight decline, largely impacted by profit-taking in high-flying AI-linked stocks. Market participants are closely monitoring Federal Reserve commentary and upcoming inflation data for clues on future monetary policy. Not financial advice.

items.headline: S&P 500 Performance
items.statContext: Up 0.14% (approx. 10 points) on July 2, 2026. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF ($SPY) is up 0.41%.

items.headline: NASDAQ Composite Performance
items.statContext: Up 0.22% (approx. 65 points) on July 2, 2026, despite a drag from chip stocks. Futures were down 0.1% earlier.

items.headline: Dow Jones Industrial Average Performance
items.statContext: Up 0.16% (approx. 82 points) on July 2, 2026. Futures climbed 0.2% in pre-market trading.

items.headline: Linkhome Holdings Surges
items.summary: Linkhome Holdings Inc. ($LHAI) experienced a significant surge, leading the market's gainers.

items.headline: Sadot Group Sees Triple-Digit Gains
items.summary: Sadot Group Inc. ($SDOT) climbed over 100%, making it one of the top gainers of the day.

items.headline: Palantir Technologies Rises on Analyst Upgrade
items.summary: Palantir Technologies Inc. ($PLTR) gained after DA Davidson & Co upgraded its recommendation to buy, citing a competitive advantage in the AI sector.

items.headline: Nebius Group Declines Amid Competition Concerns
items.summary: Nebius Group ($NBIS) fell sharply due to reports that Meta's potential cloud plans could intensify competition for neocloud providers.

items.headline: Shutterstock Shares Drop
items.summary: Shutterstock, Inc. ($SSTK) experienced a significant decline today.

items.headline: CoreWeave Slips on AI Cloud Competition
items.summary: CoreWeave ($CRWV) saw a notable decline as analysts highlighted competitive risks stemming from Meta's prospective AI cloud infrastructure initiatives.

items.headline: June Jobs Report Disappoints, Unemployment Rate Dips
items.summary: The Bureau of Labor Statistics reported that the US economy added a lower-than-expected 57,000 nonfarm payroll jobs in June, significantly missing the 100,000-115,000 estimate. However, the unemployment rate dipped to 4.2%, slightly below the anticipated 4.3%. Initial jobless claims for the week ending June 27 also ticked down to 215,000.

items.headline: Fed Maintains Inflation Focus Amid Easing Risks
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh reiterated the central bank's commitment to its 2% inflation target, even as he acknowledged that inflation risks have eased. Markets are currently pricing in a 70.6% likelihood that the Federal Reserve will hold interest rates steady at its July meeting.

items.headline: Tech and Semiconductor Sectors Under Pressure; Cyclical Stocks See Inflows
items.summary: The technology and semiconductor sectors continued to face selling pressure and profit-taking today, leading to declines in several prominent chip-related stocks. Conversely, cyclical sectors such as industrials, financials, and basic materials are attracting investor interest, with some sector-specific ETFs showing gains.

items.headline: Q2 Earnings Season Approaches: Major Companies Set to Report
items.summary: The second-quarter earnings season is set to kick off in mid-July, with major companies like PepsiCo (July 9) and Delta Air Lines (July 10) leading the way, followed by key financial institutions. Later in the month, technology giants including Alphabet, Microsoft, Meta Platforms, and Apple are expected to release their results. MSC Industrial Direct Co. ($MSM) reported strong Q3 fiscal 2026 earnings, surpassing analyst estimates.
